description: Global Intelligence & Market Signal Dashboard — real-time conflict monitoring, market signals, and geopolitical risk assessment

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and dark layout shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js 14 project initialized with Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and the dark-themed layout shell.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14 App Router project with TypeScript created via create-next-app
Tailwind CSS configured with dark theme as default
shadcn/ui installed and configured (dark mode)
Root layout: dark background (#0a0a0f), sans-serif font, full viewport height

Header bar: 'SITUATION ROOM' title in bold caps, subtitle 'Global Intelligence Dashboard', placeholder threat level badge (red pulsing dot + 'CRITICAL'), multi-timezone clocks (Bangkok, UTC, Tehran, DC) updating every second, last-updated timestamp
Bottom ticker bar: horizontally scrolling placeholder text, dark semi-transparent background, fixed to bottom
Main content area: CSS grid layout with named areas ready for panels
App runs with npm run dev on port 3000
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Bloomberg terminal aesthetic. Use Inter or JetBrains Mono for data. Muted borders (#1a1a2e), accent colors for data points. Header should feel like a command center.

userStories.title: Conflict map with strike locations, shipping routes, and naval markers
userStories.description: As an executive, I want an interactive map showing the conflict zone, strike locations, naval positions, and shipping routes.
acceptanceCriteria: react-leaflet installed with dark map tiles (CartoDB dark_all)
Map centered on Middle East / Persian Gulf (lat 28, lng 54, zoom 5)
Strike markers (red/orange pulsing circles): Tehran, Isfahan nuclear facility, Bushehr, Shiraz IRGC base, Parchin, Natanz, Fordow
Retaliation markers (yellow): Tel Aviv, Dubai Airport, Abu Dhabi, Jebel Ali Port
Strait of Hormuz chokepoint: red semi-transparent polygon overlay
Shipping routes: normal route through Hormuz (red dashed polyline), alternative Cape of Good Hope route (green polyline)
Port markers (blue): Jebel Ali, Fujairah, Ras Tanura, Bandar Abbas, Chabahar
US naval markers (blue stars): estimated carrier group positions in Arabian Sea / Gulf of Oman
Map legend explaining all marker types
Dynamic import (no SSR) for Leaflet
All marker data in a separate constants/map-data.ts file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pulsing animation on strike markers for visual impact. Keep map data easily updatable.

userStories.title: Situation brief panel
userStories.description: As an executive, I want a concise intelligence briefing summary so I can grasp the situation in 30 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel positioned top-left, beside the map
Header: 'SITUATION BRIEF' in monospace with timestamp and 'CLASSIFIED' watermark style
Threat level indicator: colored badge (CRITICAL=red, HIGH=orange, ELEVATED=yellow)
Content: 5-6 bullet points covering current conflict status
Key stats displayed inline: operation name, leadership status, strike count, retaliation status, Hormuz status, US casualties, diplomatic signals
All briefing content in a separate config/situation-data.ts file for easy manual updates
Styled with slight monospace touches, left-aligned, compact
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use real current situation data. Operation Shield of Judah, Khamenei eliminated, Hormuz closed, UAE airports hit, 3 US KIA. Must feel like reading an actual intel brief.

userStories.title: Market signals — energy and commodity price cards with sparklines
userStories.description: As an executive, I want real-time energy and commodity prices with trend indicators to assess market exposure.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable PriceCard component: name, current price, unit, 24h change (% + absolute), trend arrow (up/down), color coding (red=price up, green=price down), mini 7-point sparkline using Recharts
API route /api/market-data that attempts Yahoo Finance API or Alpha Vantage, with hardcoded realistic fallback data
Energy section: Brent Crude (~$105/bbl), WTI (~$101/bbl), Natural Gas (~$4.8/MMBtu)
Commodities section: Gold (~$2,180/oz), Aluminum (~$2,450/t), Wheat (~$7.2/bu), Sugar (~$0.28/lb), Palm Oil (~$4,100/t), Copper (~$9,200/t)
Section headers for Energy and Commodities
Responsive grid layout for cards
Auto-refresh every 60 seconds with subtle fade animation on update
Loading skeleton states
Fallback data includes realistic sparkline arrays showing upward trends for energy/gold
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Crisis-adjusted prices. Energy and gold trending up, industrial commodities volatile. Sparklines should visually tell the story of recent volatility.

userStories.title: Currency and market indices cards
userStories.description: As an executive, I want currency rates and key market indices to see the financial ripple effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Currency section: THB/USD (~35.8), EUR/USD (~1.07), CNY/USD (~7.28), JPY/USD (~152.4) using PriceCard component
Indices section: S&P 500 (~4,850, down), SET Index (~1,320, down), Nikkei 225 (~37,200, down)
API route /api/forex that attempts free forex API with hardcoded fallback
Each card shows: current value, daily change %, sparkline
Cards arranged in grid alongside energy/commodities from US-004
Section headers for Currencies and Indices
Same 60s auto-refresh as US-004
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: During conflict, equities generally down, USD strengthens, safe havens (gold, JPY) up. THB weakening against USD due to oil import costs.

userStories.title: Supply chain risk assessment matrix
userStories.description: As an executive, I want a risk assessment across supply chain categories to identify the highest-risk areas.
acceptanceCriteria: Risk panel with 6 categories: Energy & Fuel Costs, Shipping & Logistics, Raw Materials, Packaging Materials, Currency Exposure, Consumer Demand
Each category card shows: risk level (1-5), label (Critical/High/Elevated/Moderate/Low), trend arrow, 1-2 sentence impact description
Color coding: red=Critical(5), orange=High(4), yellow=Elevated(3), blue=Moderate(2), green=Low(1)
Overall composite risk score displayed prominently with large number and label
Risk data in config/risk-data.ts: Energy=5↑, Shipping=5↑, Packaging=4↑, Raw Materials=3↑, Currency=4↑, Consumer Demand=2→
Panel header: 'SUPPLY CHAIN RISK ASSESSMENT'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core value prop for enterprise buyers. Make it sharp and scannable.

userStories.title: Scenario analysis cards
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see three scenario projections to plan for different outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Three cards in a row: Base Case, Escalation, De-escalation
Each card: scenario name, probability badge, 2-3 sentence description, impact table (energy cost %, raw materials %, freight rates %, duration estimate)
Visual distinction: Base=amber border/accent, Escalation=red, De-escalation=green
Base Case: 55% probability, Hormuz partially restricted 2-4 weeks, energy +15-25%, freight +30-50%
Escalation: 30% probability, full Hormuz closure 1-3 months, energy +40-80%, freight +100-200%
De-escalation: 15% probability, ceasefire in 2 weeks, energy -10%, freight normalizes 4-6 weeks
Data in config/scenarios.ts
Panel header: 'SCENARIO ANALYSIS'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Professional report feel. These probabilities reflect current situation with Hormuz effectively closed and no diplomatic off-ramp yet.

userStories.title: Live intelligence news feed with category filters
userStories.description: As an executive, I want a live feed of conflict-related news filtered by category.
acceptanceCriteria: API route /api/news that fetches from RSS feeds (Al Jazeera, Reuters, BBC) using rss-parser, with hardcoded fallback headlines
News panel showing scrollable list of 20 latest items
Each item: relative timestamp, source badge, headline text, category tag (Military/Economic/Shipping/Diplomatic/Sanctions)
Category filter buttons at top: All, Military, Economic, Shipping, Diplomatic, Sanctions
Auto-categorization based on keywords in headlines
Auto-refresh every 5 minutes
Fallback: 15-20 realistic hardcoded headlines with today's date covering the Iran conflict
Panel header: 'LIVE INTEL FEED' with pulsing green dot
Scrollable with max height, newest first
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RSS feeds to try: https://www.aljazeera.com/xml/rss/all.xml, http://feeds.reuters.com/reuters/worldNews, http://feeds.bbci.co.uk/news/world/rss.xml. Filter for conflict-related keywords.

userStories.title: Final layout, ticker bar, animations, Dockerfile, and polish
userStories.description: As an executive viewing on a war room display, I want a polished, cohesive dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid layout finalized: top row = situation brief (left 30%) + map (right 70%); middle = market signals (full width grid of cards); lower = risk matrix (left) + scenarios (right); bottom section = news feed (full width); fixed bottom = ticker bar
Bottom ticker bar populated with live data: Brent price, WTI, Gold, THB/USD, S&P 500, SET, Hormuz status, threat level — scrolling horizontally
Header threat level dynamically calculated from risk matrix composite score
Subtle animations: number transitions on price updates, fade-in on data load, pulsing on critical indicators
No console errors or warnings in production build
Dockerfile for containerized deployment (multi-stage: build + nginx or standalone Next.js)
README.md: project overview, screenshot placeholder, setup instructions, environment variables, data source docs, deployment guide
.env.example with all optional API keys documented
npm run build succeeds without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the polish pass. Should look like a $50K consulting deliverable on a 1920x1080+ display. Commit everything, push branch to origin.
